comment.name: Comment
comment.text:
Afternoon Report


Last Updated: 3/14/26 | 1:26PM

Good afternoon, Sunday River!

Whether you're out carving the fresh snow from this morning or getting an early start on après at the Iglu, today has been a real gem. Four inches of fresh snow is not a bad mid-March treat.

This afternoon we're spinning lifts across all eight peaks until 4PM, when South Ridge takes over for the final Twilight Ski of the season from 4 to 6PM.

Looking ahead to tomorrow, our grooming team will be out refreshing the mountain and setting us up with a clean canvas for morning turns. First chair spins at 8AM sharp, so don't miss it.

The time has come to renew your New England Pass for the 2026/27 season. If you don't have one yet this season, you can still grab one at the lowest price of the year, and as an added bonus, you'll be able to ski the rest of this season too. Either way, now is the time to buy and get the most bang for your buck.

And finally, a heads-up for passholders. This weekend we're dropping seriously good deals across our retail shops, with up to 60% off sales at Sunday River Sports, the KÜHL Store at the Summit, Aster & Birch, the Jordan Gift Shop, and Betty's Treats & Treasures.

We'll see you out there!

-Team Snow

comment.name: Comment 2
comment.text:
Good morning, Sunday River!

Happy Pi Day. Come grab a slice of the powder pie. Overnight we picked up about 3 inches of light, fluffy snow, covering all eight peaks and giving the mountain a nice refresh. It's still snowing at a pretty good clip right now, though things should slow down as the sun comes up. After that, snow showers will continue rolling through during the day, and forecasts are calling for up to two more inches by tonight.

Temps this morning around the resort are sitting at 22 at Locke Summit, 23 at North Peak, 25 in Jordan Basin, and 28 at White Cap Base. Temperatures will climb about ten degrees through the day, topping out around 33 at the summits and 40 down in the parking lots.

Today we've got 97 trails open across 554 acres of terrain, and between the new snow and the hard work from the grooming team this week, it's all skiing really well out there.

First chair spins at 8AM, delivering folks to the goods until 4PM, before South Ridge takes over from 4 to 6PM for the final Twilight Ski of the season.

If the snow isn't enough reason to be here, we've also got a stacked lineup of fun on deck today, including a Bissell Brothers takeover party at the Igloo, with DJ NDB throwing down the beats.

In other news, the time has come to renew your New England Pass for the 2026/27 season. If you don't have one yet this year, you can still grab one at the lowest price of the year, and as a bonus, you'll be able to ski the rest of this season too. Either way, now is the time to lock one in and get the most bang for your buck.

And finally, a heads up for passholders. This weekend we're dropping seriously good deals with up to 60% off across our retail shops. You'll find sales at Sunday River Sports, the KÜHL Store at the Summit, Aster & Birch, the Jordan Gift Shop, and Betty's Treats & Treasures.

We'll see you out there!

-Team Snow
